summary: Context engineering is the discipline of designing how contextual information is gathered, structured, ranked, governed, and delivered to AI systems. This knowledge map describes the core concepts, their relationships, and key implementations.

discipline.name: Context Engineering
discipline.description: The overarching discipline concerned with context for AI systems

architecture.name: Enterprise Context Fabric
architecture.description: The architectural pattern that implements context engineering at enterprise scale

layers.name: Context Ingestion Layer
layers.description: Connects to source systems and extracts signals

layers.name: Context Assembly Layer
layers.description: Structures, ranks, and combines signals into context objects

layers.name: Context Delivery Layer
layers.description: Delivers assembled context to AI systems

key_concepts.name: Context Capsules
key_concepts.description: Structured packages for delivering context to AI systems

key_concepts.name: Enterprise AI Memory
key_concepts.description: Durable storage of context across sessions and workflows

key_concepts.name: Time-to-Context
key_concepts.description: Performance metric for context delivery latency

key_concepts.name: Context Ingestion
key_concepts.description: Extraction and normalization of signals from source systems

key_concepts.name: Context Delivery
key_concepts.description: Transmission of assembled context to AI consumers
